title: Industrial policy for the Intelligence Age
optimized_headline: Harnessing Industrial Policy for the Future of Artificial Intelligence
raw_excerpt: Explore our ambitious, people-first industrial policy ideas for the AI era—focused on expanding opportunity, sharing prosperity, and building resilient institutions as advanced intelligence evolves.
raw_body: Explore our ambitious, people-first industrial policy ideas for the AI era—focused on expanding opportunity, sharing prosperity, and building resilient institutions as advanced intelligence evolves.
speedrun: A new industrial policy for the AI era aims to create opportunities and share prosperity while building strong institutions. This approach emphasizes a people-first strategy as advanced intelligence continues to develop. Key specifics include a focus on resilience and inclusivity in the workforce. This matters now because as AI technologies evolve, they could significantly reshape job markets and societal structures.
why_it_matters: Workers in tech and manufacturing could see immediate benefits from policies that prioritize job creation and training.
This approach suggests a broader market shift towards integrating AI responsibly, emphasizing equity and sustainability in economic growth.

lenses.eli12: The new industrial policy is like planting seeds in a garden. It aims to grow opportunities for everyone, not just a few. By focusing on people, it hopes to ensure that as AI grows, everyone can benefit. This is important for everyday people because it could lead to more jobs and fairer access to technology.
lenses.pm: For product managers and founders, this policy highlights the need to align product development with social responsibility. Understanding user needs in a changing job landscape could guide better product strategies. This focus on inclusivity may also lower costs by tapping into a broader talent pool and fostering innovation.
lenses.engineer: From a technical perspective, this policy encourages the development of AI systems that are not only efficient but also equitable. It suggests that engineers should consider the societal impact of their designs, potentially leading to benchmarks that prioritize fairness alongside performance. This could shape future AI models to be more inclusive and resilient.
